events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes an HVAC-R charging & diagnostics task on a commercial project. The observed field condition is "sight-glass bubbles misread." The work is through its testing/inspection step. Reference material: mfr charging charts.

Sight-glass bubbles indicate refrigerant charge status. If bubbles are present, it could mean undercharge, but bubbles can also be misread due to other factors (e.g., flash gas, temperature, pressure drops). "Sight-glass bubbles misread" suggests the technician misinterpreted the sight glass, leading to incorrect charging. This is a diagnostic error. The work is through testing/inspection, so if the charge is wrong due to misreading, the system is likely improperly charged, which is non-compliant/defective.

Decision: fail (improper charge due to misreading sight glass).
Risk: medium (improper charge can cause efficiency issues, compressor damage).
Work stage: tested/inspected.
Component conditions: installed-defective (system improperly charged), non-compliant.
Percent complete: maybe 90 (tested but needs rework).
Value: 0 (no numeric reading given).
Confidence: 0.8.
Findings: sight-glass bubbles misread, leading to incorrect refrigerant charge.
Actions: re-evaluate charge using mfr charging charts, verify subcooling/superheat, correct charge.
Rationale: misreading sight glass leads to improper charge; must follow manufacturer charging charts.
References: mfr charging charts.
